back_translation: The two angels came to Sodom in the evening while Lot was sitting at the city gate. When Lot saw them, he rose to greet them and bowed down to the ground.

He said, "My lords, please turn aside to your servant's house; wash your feet and stay the night, then rise early and continue your journey." But they answered, "No, we will spend the night in the square."
But Lot urged them strongly, so they turned aside to him and entered his house. He prepared a feast for them and baked unleavened bread, and they ate.
Before they had gone to bed, the men of the city, the men of Sodom, both young and old, surrounded the house — all the people from every corner.
They called out to Lot, saying, "Where are the men who came to you tonight? Send them out to us so we can have sexual relations with them."
Lot went out to them at the entrance and shut the door behind him.
He said, "Please, my brothers, do not do such evil."

"Behold, I have two daughters who have not had relations with any man. Let me bring them out to you, and do with them as seems good in your eyes. But to these men do nothing, for they have come under the shelter of my roof."

They said, "Get out of the way!" And they said, "This one came as a foreigner, and he keeps acting like a judge! Now we will treat you worse than them." And they pressed hard against Lot and moved in to break down the door.
But the men reached out their hands, brought Lot inside the house with them, and shut the door.
And they struck the men at the entrance of the house with blindness, both small and great, so that they wearied themselves trying to find the door.
The men said to Lot, "Whom else do you have here? A son-in-law, your sons, your daughters, or anyone else in the city who belongs to you — get them out of this place,
because we are going to destroy this place. The outcry against them before the LORD has grown great, and the LORD has sent us to destroy it."

So Lot went out and spoke to his sons-in-law who were to take his daughters, and said, "Get up, leave this place, for the LORD is about to destroy the city." But he was like one joking in the eyes of his sons-in-law.
When the dawn came up, the angels urged Lot, saying, "Get up, take your wife and your two daughters who are here, lest you be swept away in the punishment of the city."

But he hesitated, so the men grasped his hand, and the hand of his wife, and the hand of his two daughters — through the LORD's compassion upon him — and they brought him out and set him outside the city.
When they had brought them out, one said, "Flee for your life! Do not look behind you, and do not stop anywhere on the plain. Flee to the mountains, lest you be swept away."
But Lot said to them, "No, please, my Lord!"

"Behold, your servant has found favor in your sight, and you have magnified your kindness which you have shown me by preserving my life. But I cannot flee to the mountains; the disaster will overtake me, and I will die."
"Look, this town is near to flee to, and it is small. Let me flee there — is it not small? — and my life will be saved."
And he said to him, "Behold, I have lifted up your face for this matter also, and I will not overthrow the town of which you have spoken."
"Hurry, flee there, for I cannot do anything until you arrive there." Therefore the name of the town was called Zoar.
The sun had risen over the land when Lot came to Zoar.
Then the LORD rained sulfur and fire on Sodom and on Gomorrah — from the LORD, from the heavens.
And He overthrew these cities and the entire plain, and all the inhabitants of the cities, and the vegetation of the ground.
But his wife looked back from behind him, and she became a pillar of salt.
Abraham rose early in the morning to the place where he had stood before the LORD.
He looked over the face of Sodom and Gomorrah, and over all the face of the land of the plain, and he saw, and behold, the smoke of the land went up like the smoke of a furnace.
When God destroyed the cities of the plain, God remembered Abraham, and He sent Lot out from the midst of the catastrophe in the overthrowing of the cities where Lot had lived.
Lot went up from Zoar and lived in the mountains, with his two daughters with him, because he was afraid to live in Zoar. And he lived in the cave, he and his two daughters.
The firstborn said to the younger, "Our father is old, and there is no man in the land to come in to us according to the way of all the earth."
"Come, let us make our father drink wine, and let us lie with him, that we may preserve seed from our father."
So they made their father drink wine that night, and the firstborn went in and lay with her father; and he was not aware when she lay down or when she rose.

On the next day, the firstborn said to the younger, "Behold, I lay last night with my father. Let us make him drink wine again tonight, and you go in and lie with him, that we may preserve seed from our father."
So they made their father drink wine that night also, and the younger arose and lay with him; and he was not aware when she lay down or when she rose.
Thus the two daughters of Lot conceived by their father.
The firstborn bore a son and called his name Moab; he is the father of the Moabites to this day.
And the younger, she also bore a son, and called his name Ben-ammi; he is the father of the sons of Ammon to this day.
